sessions.intent: Migrate hand-built goal/step executor tests in PLang.Tests/Modules to load goals through the real read path (TestApp.Create + Make.Goal + RealGoalLoad.ViaChannel).

before.assumptions: Baseline PLang.Tests/Modules is green (failed: 0). Executor tests are those calling _app.RunGoalAsync or step(s).RunAsync on a hand-built goal/step; handler-direct .Run()/action.RunAsync and provider/fold isolation tests stay.
before.risk: ViaChannel serialize->read round-trip may expose a real read-path gap on some param shape (e.g. goal.call goalname dict). If so, revert that file and report.

actions.context: Migrated step.RunAsync executor tests: new app->TestApp.Create; runner step built via Make.Goal + RealGoalLoad.ViaChannel, capture goal.Steps.First() then step.RunAsync. foreach itemname/keyname -> Make.Param(...,'variable'). Empty target goals (DoNothing/Noop/ProcessItem) and the registered Inner fixture left as new Goal. The 3 action.RunAsync-direct foreach tests (Empty/Null/Cancellation) left as-is.

StepsSubStep: Step-returning helpers -> Make.StepDef helpers + LoadSteps() routes through ViaChannel, returning goal.Steps for steps.RunAsync (and HasIndentedChildren). IfErrorOrchestration: removed now-unused Action alias; both step.RunAsync tests load via ViaChannel.

3 RunGoalAsync executor tests (Read_UnregisteredScheme, Integration_FileExists, Integration_FileNotExists) now build via Make.Goal + ViaChannel; dropped manual StampTemplates walk. variable.set Name -> Make.Param('Name',...,'variable'). All .Run() handler-direct tests left as-is. Constructor new app -> TestApp.Create.

Examined, deliberately left: all execute via handler .Run()/action.RunAsync/modifiers.RunAsync/_provider.Render — isolation tests, not RunGoalAsync/steps.RunAsync executors. RenderTests goals are callGoal registry fixtures, never directly run.

after.notes: PLang.Tests/Modules builds clean (no error CS) and full suite passes (failed: 0). No read-path gaps exposed; nothing reverted. Not committed (per task).
